description: A part is just as 'or-some' as the whole.

hints.text: The goal is $(A \land B) \to (A \lor C)$. You only need $A$ to make $A \lor C$ true — $B$ and $C$ are along for the ride. The trick is to extract $A$ from the conjunction first, then use $\lor$-introduction.
Extract $A$ from the left side of the conjunction. $B$ is true too, but we won't need it.
Now you have $A$. Use `or-intro-l` to form $A \lor C$ — slot $A$ on the left and let $C$ be whatever it wants on the right.
Discharge the assumption to complete the implication.
